---
title: "Parenthood, Immigration and Further Education"
subtitle: "Analyses and Visualizations"
author: "Nate Breznau, Johannes Bonnes and Sylvia Rahn"
output:
  word_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '1'
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    toc_depth: 1
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '1'
---

NOTE: Linking error is necessary to compare SE across cycles. See ChatGPT output for a reccomendation for how to integrate it. 

## Setup   

```{r setup, echo = F, warning = F, message = F}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

packages <- c('tidyverse',
              'readr',
              'knitr',
              'here',
              'ggplot2',
              'ggrepel',
              'ragg',
              'kableExtra',
              'countrycode',
              'readxl',
              'jtools',
              'lme4',
              'lmerTest',
              'broom.mixed',
              'viridis',
              'survey',
              'wbstats')

pacman::p_load(packages, character.only = T)

```

## Data

```{r }


df <- read_rds(here("Data", "df.RDS")) 
df2 <- read_rds(here("Data", "df2.RDS")) 

# basic or lower literacy
df2 <- df2 %>%
  mutate(reading_low = ifelse(literacy_level %in% c(0, 1), 1, 0),
         ALL = 1)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(reading_low = ifelse(literacy_level %in% c(0, 1), 1, 0),
         ALL = 1)

```

## Regressions

### Basic

We do not use the weighting variables here because we are focusing in on specific groups, and the weights are for the full population.


```{r}
df_fem_imm <- subset(df2, FORBORN == 1 & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORLANG) & !is.na(ed3) & !is.na(ed_currently) & !is.na(female))

df_fem_nat <- subset(df2, FORBORN == 0 & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORLANG) & !is.na(ed3) & !is.na(ed_currently) & !is.na(female))

m1_imm_1 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + female + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + factor(iso3c), data = df_fem_imm)

m1_nat_1 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + female + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + factor(iso3c), data = df_fem_nat)

m1_imm_2 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + female + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + ed_currently + factor(iso3c), data = df_fem_imm)

m1_nat_2 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + female + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + ed_currently + factor(iso3c), data = df_fem_nat)

export_summs(
  m1_imm_1, m1_nat_1, m1_imm_2, m1_nat_2,
  model.names = c("Foreign-born 1", "Native-born 1", "Foreign-born 2", "Native-born 2"),
  coefs = c(
    "(Intercept)",
    "NFE12",
    "female",
    "children",
    "FORLANG",
    "factor(ed3)2",
    "factor(ed3)3",
    "ed_currently"
  ),
  to.file = "html",
  file.name = here("Results", "Reg_Table_1.html")
)

include_graphics(here("Results", "Reg_Table_1.html"))

```
### Test for Sample Bias

Here we correlate the residuals with the weighting variable. If this is close to zero then we can look at our sample as a representative treatment for ALE and literacy. 

```{r}

df_fem_imm$resid_imm_1 <- residuals(m1_imm_1)
df_fem_nat$resid_nat_1 <- residuals(m1_nat_1)
df_fem_imm$resid_imm_2 <- residuals(m1_imm_2)
df_fem_nat$resid_nat_2 <- residuals(m1_nat_2)

testcor1 <- cor(df_fem_imm$aweight, df_fem_imm$resid_imm_1)
testcor2 <- cor(df_fem_imm$aweight, df_fem_imm$resid_imm_2)
testcor3 <- cor(df_fem_nat$aweight, df_fem_nat$resid_nat_1)
testcor4 <- cor(df_fem_nat$aweight, df_fem_nat$resid_nat_2)

```

Correlations are `r round(testcor1,2`, `r round(testcor2,2`, `r round(testcor3,2`, & `r round(testcor4,2`. This suggests that there is no need to weight the sample.

### Multilevel

#### Recodes

```{r}


df2 <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    ed3_secondary = ifelse(ed3 == 2, 1, 0),
    ed3_tertiary = ifelse(ed3 == 3, 1, 0)
  )

# within/between decomposition
df2 <- df2 %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  mutate(
    # weighted means are necessary at the country level
    NFE12_between = sum(SPFWT0 * NFE12, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(NFE12)], na.rm = TRUE),
    female_between = sum(SPFWT0 * female, na.rm = T) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(female)], na.rm = T),
    children_between = sum(SPFWT0 * children, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(children)], na.rm = TRUE),
    ed_currently_between = sum(SPFWT0 * ed_currently, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(ed_currently)], na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_secondary_between = sum(SPFWT0 * ed3_secondary, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(ed3_secondary)], na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_tertiary_between = sum(SPFWT0 * ed3_tertiary, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(ed3_tertiary)], na.rm = TRUE),
    forborn_between = sum(SPFWT0 * FORLANG, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(FORLANG)], na.rm = TRUE),
    forlang_between = sum(SPFWT0 * FORBORN, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(FORBORN)], na.rm = TRUE),
    NFE12_within = NFE12 - NFE12_between,
    children_within = children - children_between,
    female_within = female - female_between,
    ed_currently_within = ed_currently - ed_currently_between,
    ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary - ed3_secondary_between,
    ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary - ed3_tertiary_between,
    forborn_within = FORBORN - forborn_between,
    forlang_within = FORLANG - forlang_between
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


# the within effects need to be further centered by group
df2_imm <- df2 %>%
  subset(FORBORN == 1) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  mutate(
    NFE12_within = NFE12_within - mean(NFE12_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    female_within = female_within - mean(female_within, na.rm = T),
    children_within = children_within - mean(children_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed_currently_within = ed_currently_within - mean(ed_currently_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary_within - mean(ed3_secondary_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary_within - mean(ed3_tertiary_within, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

df2_nat <- df2 %>%
  subset(FORBORN == 0) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  mutate(
    NFE12_within = NFE12_within - mean(NFE12_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    children_within = children_within - mean(children_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    female_within = female_within - mean(female_within, na.rm = T),
    ed_currently_within = ed_currently_within - mean(ed_currently_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary_within - mean(ed3_secondary_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary_within - mean(ed3_tertiary_within, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

```


#### Models

We first look at immigrants and native-born separately. Then we combine them to determine if there is an 'immigrant penalty' after controlling for participation in further education and other variables. 

```{r}

m1_imm_mlm <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    female_within + female_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = df2_imm
)

ranef_list <- ranef(m1_imm_mlm)

# Get country-level grand intercept deviations
iso3c_intercepts <- ranef_list$iso3c



m1_nat_mlm <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    female_within + female_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = df2_nat
)

m2 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    female_within + female_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    forborn_within + forborn_between +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2)
)

m3 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    female_within + female_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    forborn_within + forborn_between +
    forborn_within*NFE12_within +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2)
)

```

#### Results


```{r}

export_summs(
  m1_imm_mlm, m1_nat_mlm, m2, m3,
  model.names = c("Foreign-born", "Native-born", "Pooled", "Pooled with ALE interaction"),
  to.file = "html",
  file.name = here("Results", "Reg_Table_2.html"
))

```



### ATEs

Type 1: Foreign-born mother, non-native speaker
c(forborn_within = 1, children_within = 1, forlang_within = 1)

Type 2: Foreign-born woman without children, non-native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 1, children_within = 0, forlang_within = 1)

Type 3: Foreign-born mother, native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 1, children_within = 1, forlang_within = 0)

Type 4: Foreign-born woman without children, native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 1, children_within = 0, forlang_within = 0)

Type 5: Native-born mother, native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 0, children_within = 1, forlang_within = 0)

Type 6: Native-born woman without children, native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 0, children_within = 0, forlang_within = 0)
)

#### T S2. Interactive GLM

Here we interact all intersectional variables of interest with ALE (NFE12) to see the unique effects if any.

The correlation between foreign-born and foreign-language is not so high as to warrant dropping one.

select(df2, FORBORN, FORLANG) %>% cor(., use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
          FORBORN   FORLANG
FORBORN 1.0000000 0.5645113
FORLANG 0.5645113 1.0000000

```{r}
m4 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    female_within + female_between + 
    children_within + children_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    forborn_within + forborn_between +
    # interaction with all sub-interactions
    female_within*forborn_within*children_within*forlang_within*NFE12_within +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2)
)


export_summs(
  m4,
  coefs = c(
    "(Intercept)" = "(Intercept)",
    "ALE (within)" = "NFE12_within",
    "ALE (between)" = "NFE12_between",
    "Female (within)" = "female_within",
    "Female (between)" = "female_between",
    "Children (within)" = "children_within",
    "Children (between)" = "children_between",
    "Non-native speaker (within)" = "forlang_within",
    "Non-native speaker (between)" = "forlang_between",
    "Foreign-born (within)" = "forborn_within",
    "Foreign-born (between)" = "forborn_between",
    "Secondary education (within)" = "ed3_secondary_within",
    "Secondary education (between)" = "ed3_secondary_between",
    "Tertiary education (within)" = "ed3_tertiary_within",
    "Tertiary education (between)" = "ed3_tertiary_between",
    "GDP per capita" = "gdppc",
    "Female x Foreign-born (within)" = "female_within:forborn_within",
    "Female x Children (within)" = "female_within:children_within",
    "Children x Foreign-born (within)" = "children_within:forborn_within",      
    "Female x Foreign-language (within)" = "female_within:forlang_within",  
    "Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "forlang_within:forborn_within", 
    "Children x Foreign-language (within)" = "children_within:forlang_within",
    "ALE x Female (within)" =  "NFE12_within:female_within",        
    "ALE x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Children (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within",                 
    "ALE x Foreign-language (within)" = "NFE12_within:forlang_within",    
    "Female x Children x Foreign-born" = "female_within:children_within:forborn_within",  
    "Female x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "female_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",  
    "Female x Children x Foreign-language (within)" = "female_within:children_within:forlang_within",
    "Children x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Female x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:forborn_within", 
    "ALE x Female x Children (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:children_within",
    "ALE x Children x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within:forborn_within",  
    "ALE x Female x Foreign-language (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:forlang_within",  
    "ALE x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Children x Foreign-language (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within:forlang_within",
    "Female x Children x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "female_within:children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within", 
    "ALE x Female x Children x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:children_within:forborn_within",    
    "ALE x Female x Foreign-language x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Female x Children x Foreign-language (within)" = "NFE12_within:female_within:children_within:forlang_within",   
    "ALE x Children x Foreign-language x Foreign-born" = "NFE12_within:children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Female x Children x Foreign-language x Foreign-born" = "NFE12_within:female_within:children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within"
  ),
  to.file = "html",
  file.name = here("Results", "Reg_Table_S2.html")
)

include_graphics(here("Results", "Reg_Table_S2.html"))
```
#### Predictions

```{r}

df2r <- subset(df2, !is.na(female) & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children_within) & !is.na(forborn_within) & !is.na(forlang_within) & !is.na(ed3) & !is.na(gdppc) & !is.na(skill_reading))



#Type 1: Foreign-born mother, non-native speaker
df_type10 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type11 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 1)

df_type10m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type11m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 2: Foreign-born woman without children, non-native
df_type20 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type21 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 1)

df_type20m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type21m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 1)


#Type 3: Foreign-born mother, native speaker
df_type30 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type31 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

df_type30m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type31m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 4: Foreign-born woman without children, native speaker
df_type40 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type41 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

df_type40m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type41m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 5: Native-born mother, native speaker
df_type50 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type51 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

df_type50m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type51m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 6: Native-born woman without children, native speaker
df_type60 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type61 <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 1,
         forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

df_type60m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type61m <- df2r %>%
  mutate(female_within = 0,
         forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

# make predictions

pred10 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type10, re.form = NA)
pred11 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type11, re.form = NA)
pred10m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type10m, re.form = NA)
pred11m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type11m, re.form = NA)
pred20 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type20, re.form = NA)
pred21 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type21, re.form = NA)
pred20m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type20m, re.form = NA)
pred21m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type21m, re.form = NA)
pred30 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type30, re.form = NA)
pred31 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type31, re.form = NA)
pred30m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type30m, re.form = NA)
pred31m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type31m, re.form = NA)
pred40 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type40, re.form = NA)
pred41 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type41, re.form = NA)
pred40m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type40m, re.form = NA)
pred41m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type41m, re.form = NA)
pred50 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type50, re.form = NA)
pred51 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type51, re.form = NA)
pred50m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type50m, re.form = NA)
pred51m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type51m, re.form = NA)
pred60 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type60, re.form = NA)
pred61 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type61, re.form = NA)
pred60m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type60m, re.form = NA)
pred61m <- predict(m4males, newdata = df_type61m, re.form = NA)


# Calculate average marginal effect

diff1 <- pred10 - pred11
diff2 <- pred20 - pred21
diff3 <- pred30 - pred31
diff4 <- pred40 - pred41
diff5 <- pred50 - pred51
diff6 <- pred60 - pred61

diff1m <- pred10m - pred11m
diff2m <- pred20m - pred21m
diff3m <- pred30m - pred31m
diff4m <- pred40m - pred41m
diff5m <- pred50m - pred51m
diff6m <- pred60m - pred61m




AME1 <- mean(diff1)
ttest1 <- t.test(pred11, pred10, paired = FALSE)
AME2 <- mean(diff2)
ttest2 <- t.test(pred21, pred20, paired = FALSE)
AME3 <- mean(diff3)
ttest3 <- t.test(pred31, pred30, paired = FALSE)
AME4 <- mean(diff4)
ttest4 <- t.test(pred41, pred40, paired = FALSE)
AME5 <- mean(diff5)
ttest5 <- t.test(pred51, pred50, paired = FALSE)
AME6 <- mean(diff6)
ttest6 <- t.test(pred61, pred60, paired = FALSE)

AME1m <- mean(diff1m)
ttest1m <- t.test(pred11m, pred10m, paired = FALSE)
AME2m <- mean(diff2m)
ttest2m <- t.test(pred21m, pred20m, paired = FALSE)
AME3m <- mean(diff3m)
ttest3m <- t.test(pred31m, pred30m, paired = FALSE)
AME4m <- mean(diff4m)
ttest4m <- t.test(pred41m, pred40m, paired = FALSE)
AME5m <- mean(diff5m)
ttest5m <- t.test(pred51m, pred50m, paired = FALSE)
AME6m <- mean(diff6m)
ttest6m <- t.test(pred61m, pred60m, paired = FALSE)


```


#### Plot

##### Combine values

```{r}



ame_df <- data.frame(
  type = c(
    "1. Foreign-born mother\nnon-native speaker",
    "2. Foreign-born no children\nnon-native speaker",
    "3. Foreign-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "4. Foreign-born no children\nnative speaker",
    "5. Native-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "6. Native-born no children\nnative speaker"
  ),
  AME = c(AME1, AME2, AME3, AME4, AME5, AME6),
  lower = c(ttest1$conf.int[1], ttest2$conf.int[1], ttest3$conf.int[1],
            ttest4$conf.int[1], ttest5$conf.int[1], ttest6$conf.int[1]),
  upper = c(ttest1$conf.int[2], ttest2$conf.int[2], ttest3$conf.int[2],
            ttest4$conf.int[2], ttest5$conf.int[2], ttest6$conf.int[2])
)

ame_df <- ame_df %>%
  mutate(
    AME = -AME,
    lower = lower,
    upper = upper
  )

ame_dfm <- data.frame(
  type = c(
    "1. Foreign-born father\nnon-native speaker",
    "2. Foreign-born no children\nnon-native speaker",
    "3. Foreign-born father\nnative speaker",
    "4. Foreign-born no children\nnative speaker",
    "5. Native-born father\nnative speaker",
    "6. Native-born no children\nnative speaker"
  ),
  AME = c(AME1m, AME2m, AME3m, AME4m, AME5m, AME6m),
  lower = c(ttest1m$conf.int[1], ttest2m$conf.int[1], ttest3m$conf.int[1],
            ttest4m$conf.int[1], ttest5m$conf.int[1], ttest6m$conf.int[1]),
  upper = c(ttest1m$conf.int[2], ttest2m$conf.int[2], ttest3m$conf.int[2],
            ttest4m$conf.int[2], ttest5m$conf.int[2], ttest6m$conf.int[2])
)

ame_dfm <- ame_dfm %>%
  mutate(
    AME = -AME,
    lower = lower,
    upper = upper
  )



```

##### Fig 1f orig CIs

Original CIs

```{r}

agg_png(here("Results", "Fig1f.png"), res = 144, height = 600, width = 750)

ggplot(ame_df, aes(x = type, y = AME, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", begin = 0.1, end = 0.9) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Treatment Effect\nof Participation in ALE",
    fill = "Type"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig1f.png"))


```

##### Fig 1m orig CIs

Original CIs

```{r}

agg_png(here("Results", "Fig1m.png"), res = 144, height = 600, width = 750)

ggplot(ame_dfm, aes(x = type, y = AME, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", begin = 0.1, end = 0.9) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Treatment Effect\nof Participation in ALE",
    fill = "Type"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig1m.png"))


```
##### Adjust CIs

The confidence intervals here refer to the entire sample across 26 countries. To make them more realistic to a treatment in one country, we adjust them down to a sample of 5,700 which is the average sample size per country.

```{r}

# Function to adjust CI to a new sample size (e.g., average country = 5700)
adjust_ci <- function(ttest_obj, new_n = 5700) {
  mean_diff <- ttest_obj$estimate[1] - ttest_obj$estimate[2]
  pooled_sd <- sqrt(((ttest_obj$parameter + 1) * ttest_obj$stderr^2))  # back-calculate SD
  se_new <- pooled_sd / sqrt(new_n)
  ci_margin <- qt(0.975, df = new_n - 1) * se_new
  lower <- mean_diff - ci_margin
  upper <- mean_diff + ci_margin
  return(c(lower, upper))
}

# Apply adjusted CI to each t-test result
ci1 <- adjust_ci(ttest1)
ci2 <- adjust_ci(ttest2)
ci3 <- adjust_ci(ttest3)
ci4 <- adjust_ci(ttest4)
ci5 <- adjust_ci(ttest5)
ci6 <- adjust_ci(ttest6)

ci1m <- adjust_ci(ttest1m)
ci2m <- adjust_ci(ttest2m)
ci3m <- adjust_ci(ttest3m)
ci4m <- adjust_ci(ttest4m)
ci5m <- adjust_ci(ttest5m)
ci6m <- adjust_ci(ttest6m)

# Construct new dataframe for plotting with updated CIs
ame_df2 <- data.frame(
  type = c(
    "1. Foreign-born mother\nnon-native speaker",
    "2. Foreign-born no children\nnon-native speaker",
    "3. Foreign-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "4. Foreign-born no children\nnative speaker",
    "5. Native-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "6. Native-born no children\nnative speaker"
  ),
  AME = c(-AME1, -AME2, -AME3, -AME4, -AME5, -AME6),  # note the negative flip as before
  lower = c(ci1[2], ci2[2], ci3[2], ci4[2], ci5[2], ci6[2]),  # upper bound becomes more negative
  upper = c(ci1[1], ci2[1], ci3[1], ci4[1], ci5[1], ci6[1])   # lower bound becomes more negative
)

ame_df2m <- data.frame(
  type = c(
    "1. Foreign-born mother\nnon-native speaker",
    "2. Foreign-born no children\nnon-native speaker",
    "3. Foreign-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "4. Foreign-born no children\nnative speaker",
    "5. Native-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "6. Native-born no children\nnative speaker"
  ),
  AME = c(-AME1m, -AME2m, -AME3m, -AME4m, -AME5m, -AME6m),  # note the negative flip as before
  lower = c(ci1m[2], ci2m[2], ci3m[2], ci4m[2], ci5m[2], ci6m[2]),  # upper bound becomes more negative
  upper = c(ci1m[1], ci2m[1], ci3m[1], ci4m[1], ci5m[1], ci6m[1])   # lower bound becomes more negative
)


```



##### Fig 1f new CIs


```{r}

agg_png(here("Results", "Fig1fnew.png"), res = 144, height = 600, width = 750)

ggplot(ame_df2, aes(x = type, y = AME, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2) +
  #annotate("text", y = c(40,40,40,40,40,40), x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6), label = c(round(AME1gap,2),round(AME2gap,2),round(AME3gap,2),round(AME4gap,2),round(AME5gap,2),round(AME6gap,2)), hjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", begin = 0.1, end = 0.9) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Treatment Effect\nof Participation in ALE",
    fill = "Type"
  ) +
  ylim(0,35) +
  coord_flip() +  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig1fnew.png"))


```

##### Fig 1f new CIs


```{r}

agg_png(here("Results", "Fig1mnew.png"), res = 144, height = 600, width = 750)

ggplot(ame_df2m, aes(x = type, y = AME, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2) +
  #annotate("text", y = c(40,40,40,40,40,40), x = c(1,2,3,4,5,6), label = c(round(AME1gap,2),round(AME2gap,2),round(AME3gap,2),round(AME4gap,2),round(AME5gap,2),round(AME6gap,2)), hjust = 1) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", begin = 0.1, end = 0.9) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Treatment Effect\nof Participation in ALE",
    fill = "Type"
  ) +
  ylim(0,35) +
  coord_flip() +  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig1mnew.png"))


```

### Population Predictions

Here we calculate how much skills would improve if every non-participant in ALE got a skill boost equal to the average in group 1 v. group 6. 

#### Predict

```{r}

# Set AMEs
AMEg1 <- -mean(pred10 - pred11)  # gain from ALE for Type 1
AMEg6 <- -mean(pred60 - pred61)  # gain from ALE for Type 6
AMEg1m <- -mean(pred10m - pred11m)  # gain from ALE for Type 1
AMEg6m <- -mean(pred60m - pred61m)  # gain from ALE for Type 6

# Boost literacy for Type 1 non-participants
df_boost <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    boost_type1 = ifelse(
      female == 1 & NFE12 == 0 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 & FORLANG == 1,
      skill_reading + AME1,
      skill_reading
    ),
    boost_type6 = ifelse(
      female == 1 & NFE12 == 0 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 0 & FORLANG == 0,
      skill_reading + AME6,
      skill_reading
    ),
    boost_type1m = ifelse(
      female == 0 & NFE12 == 0 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 & FORLANG == 1,
      skill_reading + AME1,
      skill_reading
    ),
    boost_type6m = ifelse(
      female == 0 & NFE12 == 0 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 0 & FORLANG == 0,
      skill_reading + AME6,
      skill_reading
    )
  )

# Weighted mean literacy per country
summary_by_country <- subset(df_boost, !is.na(female) & !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORBORN) & !is.na(FORLANG)) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(
    skill_base = weighted.mean(skill_reading, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    skill_type1boost = weighted.mean(boost_type1, aweight, na.rm = TRUE) - skill_base,
    skill_type6boost = weighted.mean(boost_type6, aweight, na.rm = TRUE) - skill_base,
    skill_type1boostm = weighted.mean(boost_type1m, aweight, na.rm = TRUE) - skill_base,
    skill_type6boostm = weighted.mean(boost_type6m, aweight, na.rm = TRUE) - skill_base
  )

# Total of national pop that are Type 1
type1_shares <- df2 %>%
  mutate(type1 = ifelse(
    female == 1 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 & FORLANG == 1,
    1, 0),
    type1_noale = ifelse(
      female == 1 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 & FORLANG == 1 & NFE12 == 0,
    1, 0),
    type6 = ifelse(female == 1 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 0 & FORLANG == 0, 1, 0 ),
    type6_noALE = ifelse(female == 1 & FORBORN == 0 & NFE12 == 0 & children == 0 & FORLANG == 0, 1, 0 ),
    type1m = ifelse(
    female == 0 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 & FORLANG == 1,
    1, 0),
    type1_noalem = ifelse(
      female == 0 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 & FORLANG == 1 & NFE12 == 0,
    1, 0),
    type6m = ifelse(female == 0 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 0 & FORLANG == 0, 1, 0 ),
    type6_noALEm = ifelse(female == 0 & FORBORN == 0 & NFE12 == 0 & children == 0 & FORLANG == 0, 1, 0 )) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(
    pct_type1_totalpop = weighted.mean(type1, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_type1noale_totalpop = weighted.mean(type1_noale, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_type6_totalpop = weighted.mean(type6, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_type6_noALE_totalpop = weighted.mean(type6_noALE, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_type1_totalpopm = weighted.mean(type1m, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_type1noale_totalpopm = weighted.mean(type1_noalem, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_type6_totalpopm = weighted.mean(type6m, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_type6_noALE_totalpopm = weighted.mean(type6_noALEm, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop18to65 = sum(SPFWT0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Merge
final_df <- summary_by_country %>% 
  left_join(type1_shares, by = "iso3c") 
  

```

#### Plot

##### Females

```{r}


agg_png(here("Results", "Fig2.png"), res = 144, height = 750, width = 1000)

ggplot(final_df, aes(
  x = skill_type1boost,
  y = skill_type6boost,
  label = iso3c,
  size = pct_type1_totalpop
)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
  geom_point(aes(color = pct_type6_noALE_totalpop), alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf) +
scale_size_continuous(
  range = c(1, 6),
  name = "% Type 1\nof tot pop",
  breaks = c(0.001, 0.01, 0.03, 0.05),  
  labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
) +
  scale_color_gradient(
  name = "% Type 6\nno ALE",
  low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue",
  labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
) +
  labs(
    title = "Simulated National Literacy Gains from Targeted ALE",
    subtitle = "Boost in average female literacy if all non-participants in each group joined ALE",
    x = "Overall gain to average literacy if all type 1 without ALE\nwere given an ALE treatment effect",
    y = "Overall gain to average literacy if all type 6 without ALE\nwere given an ALE treatment effect"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig2.png"))



```

##### Males

```{r}


agg_png(here("Results", "Fig2m.png"), res = 144, height = 750, width = 1000)

ggplot(final_df, aes(
  x = skill_type1boostm,
  y = skill_type6boostm,
  label = iso3c,
  size = pct_type1_totalpopm
)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
  geom_point(aes(color = pct_type6_noALE_totalpopm), alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf) +
scale_size_continuous(
  range = c(1, 6),
  name = "% Type 1\nof tot pop",
  breaks = c(0.001, 0.01, 0.03, 0.05),  
  labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
) +
  scale_color_gradient(
  name = "% Type 6\nno ALE",
  low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue",
  labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
) +
  labs(
    title = "Simulated National Literacy Gains from Targeted ALE",
    subtitle = "Boost in average male literacy if all non-participants in each group joined ALE",
    x = "Overall gain to average literacy if all type 1 without ALE\nwere given an ALE treatment effect",
    y = "Overall gain to average literacy if all type 6 without ALE\nwere given an ALE treatment effect"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig2m.png"))



```

## Table of Group 6 and 1

```{r}

table_s3 <- final_df %>%
  mutate(oftype1_pctwithout = pct_type1noale_totalpop/pct_type1_totalpop,
         oftype6_pctwithout = pct_type6_noALE_totalpop/pct_type6_totalpop) %>%
  select(iso3c, pop18to65, pct_type1_totalpop, pct_type1noale_totalpop, oftype1_pctwithout, pct_type6_totalpop, pct_type6_noALE_totalpop, oftype6_pctwithout)
  

kable(table_s3, format = "html", digits = 3, caption = "Table S3: Population Percentages and ALE Participation") %>%
  save_kable(here("Results", "table_s3.html"))


```


## Citations

```{r}

packages %>%
  map(citation) %>%
  print(style = "text")

```


## Colophon

```{r}

sessionInfo()

```
